title: Implement API authentication and authorization for PM server endpoints
description: Add robust authentication and authorization to all PM server API endpoints. Implement API key authentication (via Authorization header) for backend integrations and session/token-based authentication for frontend users. Ensure all endpoints validate credentials before processing requests. Integrate authentication middleware in the Elysia server, and update ingress configuration to not bypass authentication. Additionally, ensure that sensitive data is never included in API responses, and enable mTLS or internal HTTPS for in-cluster communication to protect secrets in transit.
subtasks: Design and implement an authentication middleware for the PM server using Elysia, supporting API key validation for backend and session/token-based auth for frontend.
Update all PM server endpoints (GET /api/pipeline/:runId/tasks, /issues, /pr, POST /api/pipeline/intake, etc.) to require authentication and enforce authorization checks.
Update frontend to use session or token-based authentication flow for user access.
Update ingress configuration to ensure all traffic passes through authentication middleware and is not bypassed.
Implement mTLS between pods using a service mesh (e.g., Istio, Linkerd) or configure the PM server to serve HTTPS internally with a self-signed certificate.
Verify that secrets are never included in API responses to the frontend.
Test all endpoints for unauthorized access attempts and verify proper 401/403 responses.
Run OWASP ZAP scan against all PM server endpoints to validate authentication enforcement and absence of sensitive data leakage.
acceptance_criteria: All PM server API endpoints require valid authentication and return 401/403 on unauthorized requests.
Ingress does not allow unauthenticated access to any PM server endpoint.
Frontend users are authenticated via session or token-based flow.
API keys are validated for backend integrations.
mTLS or HTTPS is enabled for all in-cluster PM server communication.
No secrets or sensitive tokens are ever included in API responses.
OWASP ZAP scan shows no unauthenticated access or sensitive data exposure.
Manual attempts to access endpoints without credentials are denied.

title: Replace GitHub PAT with scoped GitHub App or fine-grained PAT and document permissions
description: Replace the use of a broad-scope GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) with a GitHub App installation token scoped only to the 5dlabs/sigma-1 repository. If a PAT must be used, create a machine user with access only to the required repo and use a fine-grained PAT with the minimum necessary permissions. Document the permissions required and ensure the secret is rotated regularly.
subtasks: Register a GitHub App with repository-only access for 5dlabs/sigma-1 and configure the PM server to use its installation token for all GitHub API operations.
If a GitHub App is not feasible, create a machine user account with access only to 5dlabs/sigma-1 and generate a fine-grained PAT scoped to that repo.
Update secret provisioning to use the new token and remove the old broad-scope PAT.
Document the minimum required permissions (e.g., contents:write, pull_requests:write) in the security documentation.
Update secret rotation policy to ensure the token is rotated at least every 90 days.
Test that the new token cannot access any repositories other than 5dlabs/sigma-1.
Verify that all GitHub API operations (branch creation, PR creation, etc.) work with the new token.
acceptance_criteria: No broad-scope PATs are used in any environment.
GitHub App installation token or fine-grained PAT is scoped only to 5dlabs/sigma-1.
Minimum required permissions are documented.
Secret rotation policy is updated and enforced.
All GitHub API operations succeed with the new token.
Attempting to use the token on other repos fails with permission denied.
Security documentation reflects the new setup.

title: Implement strict input validation and sanitization on PRD intake endpoint
description: Add strict input validation and sanitization to the POST /api/pipeline/intake endpoint. Define a JSON schema for the PRD payload, validate all incoming data, and sanitize string fields to prevent injection attacks or malformed data from propagating to downstream APIs (Linear, Hermes, GitHub, Discord). Reject any payloads that do not conform to the schema.
subtasks: Define a strict JSON schema for the PRD intake payload, specifying required fields, types, max lengths, and allowed characters.
Implement input validation using a library such as zod or typebox in the Elysia handler.
Sanitize all string fields: enforce max length, allowed characters, and strip HTML/script tags.
Reject any payloads that do not conform to the schema with a clear error message.
Add unit and integration tests for valid and invalid payloads, including edge cases and malicious input.
Verify that downstream APIs (Linear, Hermes, GitHub, Discord) cannot be exploited via crafted PRD input.
Run security tests (e.g., injection attempts) against the endpoint to confirm proper validation.
acceptance_criteria: All PRD intake requests are validated against a strict JSON schema.
Malformed or malicious payloads are rejected with 400 errors.
No HTML/script tags or dangerous content is accepted in any string field.
Downstream APIs are not affected by invalid or malicious input.
All validation and sanitization logic is covered by automated tests.
Security tests confirm no injection or bypass is possible.

title: Enforce secure storage and handling of Discord webhook URL
description: Ensure the DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL is only stored as a Kubernetes Secret and never included in any ConfigMap. Update all configuration and documentation to reflect this, and ensure all services mount the secret directly. Remove any references to the webhook URL from ConfigMaps.
subtasks: Audit all ConfigMap definitions and remove any reference to DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL.
Update all service manifests to mount DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL only from the Kubernetes Secret via secretRef or volume mount.
Update documentation and subtask 1003 to explicitly exclude DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL from the ConfigMap.
Test that the webhook URL is not present in any ConfigMap and is only accessible via the Secret.
Verify that only authorized pods can access the secret.
acceptance_criteria: DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL is not present in any ConfigMap.
All services consume the webhook URL only from the Secret.
Unauthorized pods cannot access the webhook URL.
Documentation and manifests are updated to reflect the correct handling.

title: Implement dependency pinning and vulnerability scanning for npm packages and container images
description: Pin all npm dependencies using a lockfile, specify exact container image digests, and integrate automated vulnerability scanning for both application dependencies and container images. Add automated tools (e.g., Renovate or Dependabot) for dependency update PRs.
subtasks: Update all package.json files to use exact version numbers and ensure a lockfile (package-lock.json, bun.lockb) is committed.
Configure CI to run 'npm audit' or 'bun audit' on every build and fail on high/critical vulnerabilities.
Update all container image references to use immutable digests (e.g., image@sha256:...) instead of tags.
Integrate a container image scanner (e.g., Trivy, Grype) into the CI pipeline to scan images for vulnerabilities.
Add a Renovate or Dependabot configuration file to automate dependency update PRs.
Test the pipeline with intentionally vulnerable dependencies/images to verify detection and blocking.
acceptance_criteria: All npm dependencies are pinned and a lockfile is present.
CI fails on high/critical vulnerabilities in dependencies.
All container images are referenced by digest, not tag.
CI scans images and reports vulnerabilities.
Automated dependency update PRs are created by Renovate/Dependabot.
Tests confirm detection of known vulnerabilities.

title: Enforce secure storage, access control, and retention policy for Hermes research artifacts
description: Apply access controls and encryption to the directory or storage location where raw Hermes research API responses are stored. Define and enforce a retention policy (e.g., delete after 30 days). Ensure artifacts are not publicly accessible via the frontend or any unauthenticated endpoint.
subtasks: Set filesystem permissions or object storage IAM policies to restrict access to the artifacts directory to only authorized services.
Implement encryption at rest for stored artifacts (filesystem or object storage).
Add a scheduled job or lifecycle rule to delete artifacts older than 30 days.
Audit all frontend and API endpoints to ensure artifacts are never served without authentication.
Test access controls by attempting to access artifacts from unauthorized pods or users.
acceptance_criteria: Only authorized services can access Hermes research artifacts.
Artifacts are encrypted at rest.
Artifacts older than 30 days are automatically deleted.
No unauthenticated access to artifacts is possible.
Tests confirm access controls and retention policy are enforced.

title: Harden test pod and secret rotation CronJobs to minimize credential exposure and privilege
description: Reduce the exposure window and privilege of test pods and secret rotation CronJobs. Add timeouts/TTLs to test pods, avoid logging secret values, and prefer external secret management for rotation. Restrict RBAC for rotation jobs to only necessary secrets and enforce PodSecurity standards.
subtasks: Add activeDeadlineSeconds (e.g., 300s) to test pod manifests to ensure automatic cleanup.
Modify test pod logic to check for secret existence without logging actual values.
Consider replacing test pod with a Kubernetes Job for automatic cleanup.
Add a CI step to verify no test pods remain after validation.
For secret rotation CronJobs, prefer using an External Secrets Operator with an external secret store (e.g., Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).
If CronJobs are used, enforce PodSecurityStandard (restricted profile), run as non-root, mount a read-only filesystem, and restrict RBAC to only the named secrets.
Test that test pods and rotation jobs cannot access secrets beyond their scope and are cleaned up promptly.
acceptance_criteria: Test pods have a TTL and are deleted automatically after completion.
No secret values are logged by test pods.
No test pods remain after validation.
Secret rotation jobs have minimal RBAC and run with restricted security context.
External secret management is used if feasible.
Tests confirm that jobs/pods cannot access unauthorized secrets and are cleaned up.

title: Enforce secure handling of API credentials in E2E test suite
description: Ensure the E2E test suite does not use production credentials. Create dedicated test-scoped API keys with minimal permissions for Linear, GitHub, and Discord. Add cleanup logic to remove test artifacts and consider using a separate test namespace.
subtasks: Provision test-only API credentials for Linear (limited to a test project), GitHub (scoped to a test fork or with read-only access), and Discord (test channel webhook).
Update E2E test runner configuration to use these test credentials instead of production ones.
Implement cleanup logic that runs even on test failure to delete test-created issues, PRs, and other artifacts.
Consider running E2E tests in a separate sigma1-test namespace for isolation.
Test that production data is not affected by E2E test runs and that all test artifacts are cleaned up.
acceptance_criteria: E2E tests use only test-scoped credentials.
Test credentials have minimal permissions and cannot affect production data.
All test artifacts are cleaned up after test runs.
No production issues, PRs, or notifications are created by E2E tests.
Tests confirm isolation and cleanup.
